single_external_provider.decision: Hermes should see exactly one external Brainstack memory provider.
single_external_provider.rationale: Avoids tool schema bloat and conflicting memory backends while keeping host contract intact.

shelf_separation.decision: Memory is split into profile, continuity, transcript, graph-truth, and corpus shelves.
shelf_separation.rationale: Durable facts, continuity, evidence, graph truth, and large documents need different ownership and budgets.

storage_by_responsibility.decision: Use SQLite for shell/profile/session/transcript/lexical state, Kuzu for graph truth, and Chroma for semantic corpus retrieval.
storage_by_responsibility.rationale: Keeps each storage backend scoped to the retrieval/truth job it is good at.

donor_first_native_host_seams.decision: Brainstack stays donor-first and uses Hermes native host seams instead of becoming a behavior governor.
donor_first_native_host_seams.rationale: Preserves update-safety and separates memory kernel responsibilities from host behavior policy.

workspace_truth.decision: Brainstack code and GSD planning live in Brainstack-phase50; latest Hermes source/testing lives in veglegeshermes-source.
workspace_truth.rationale: Prevents stale hermes-agent-latest planning leftovers from driving current work.

bounded_retrieval.decision: Retrieval and prompt assembly must stay bounded, route-aware, and token-disciplined.
bounded_retrieval.rationale: Brainstack should improve working memory without prompt stuffing or benchmark-only heuristics.
